Impact Evaluation in Practice

Book overview and chapter-level study-note index for Impact Evaluation in Practice.

Book notes

Impact Evaluation in Practice

Chapter-level study notes on Impact Evaluation in Practice (Gertler et al. 2016). These notes are generated from source-linked MCP research-library records and are intended for study, comparison, and later refinement.

16 chapter notes 2016 Source status: needs_review MCP evaluation-texts

Synthesis

Core Concepts

Recurring ideas and distinctions used across these chapter notes.

Map

Chapter Map

Source chapters, page ranges, and reading routes.

Practice

Practice Implications

Practical evaluation design habits drawn from the notes.

Source

2016MCP evaluation-textsStatus: needs_reviewBook overview

This index summarises generated chapter-level study notes for Paul J. Gertler, Sebastian Martinez, Patrick Premand, Laura B. Rawlings, and Christel M. J. Vermeersch’s Impact Evaluation in Practice (Gertler et al. 2016). The generated pages use selected MCP research-library retrieval records and compact evidence summaries.

Core Argument

Impact evaluation turns policy questions into causal designs by specifying the theory of change, constructing credible counterfactuals, managing implementation and ethics, and collecting data that can support defensible impact estimates.

Chapter Notes

Part 1: Introduction to Impact Evaluation

  • Chapter 1: Why Evaluate? - source pages 33-59
  • Chapter 2: Preparing for an Evaluation - source pages 61-73

Part 2: How to Evaluate

  • Chapter 3: Causal Inference and Counterfactuals - source pages 77-93
  • Chapter 4: Randomized Assignment - source pages 97-116
  • Chapter 5: Instrumental Variables - source pages 119-140
  • Chapter 6: Regression Discontinuity Design - source pages 143-157
  • Chapter 7: Difference-in-Differences - source pages 159-171
  • Chapter 8: Matching - source pages 173-188
  • Chapter 9: Addressing Methodological Challenges - source pages 191-202
  • Chapter 10: Evaluating Multifaceted Programs - source pages 205-213

Part 3: How to Implement an Impact Evaluation

  • Chapter 11: Choosing an Impact Evaluation Method - source pages 217-229
  • Chapter 12: Managing an Impact Evaluation - source pages 231-259
  • Chapter 13: The Ethics and Science of Impact Evaluation - source pages 261-275
  • Chapter 14: Disseminating Results and Achieving Policy Impact - source pages 277-287

Part 4: How to Get Data for an Impact Evaluation

  • Chapter 15: Choosing a Sample - source pages 291-319
  • Chapter 16: Finding Adequate Sources of Data - source pages 321-347

Legacy Extracts

  • What Is Impact Evaluation?
  • Constructing a Theory of Change
  • Causal Inference and Counterfactuals

References
